title: FROM scratch adds "/bin/sh -c" to the entrypoint
body:
**Description**

When using a Dockerfile `FROM scratch` or a Buildahfile `buildah from scratch`, `/bin/sh -c` it is always added to the entrypoint.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Run this Buildah based script

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -e

base_image="scratch"
image_name="test-image-from-scratch"
container_name="test-container"

# Create a build container
container="$(buildah from --name ${container_name} ${base_image})"

## Configure container metadata, labelling and entrypoint
buildah config --workingdir / "${container}"

buildah config --entrypoint ["/dummy-entrypoint"] "${container}"

buildah config --label name="${image_name}" "${container}"

## Finally saves the running container to an image
echo "==== Commit image ===="
buildah commit --rm "${container}" "${image_name}:latest"
buildah tag "${image_name}:latest" "${image_name}:$(date +%Y%m%d)"

```

2. Inspect the entrypoint

```$ buildah inspect localhost/test-image-from-scratch:latest | more
....
"OCIv1": {
        "created": "2019-08-06T17:53:44.438046162Z",
        "architecture": "amd64",
        "os": "linux",
        "config": {
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "[/dummy-entrypoint]"
            ],
            "WorkingDir": "/",
            "Labels": {
                "name": "test-image-from-scratch"
            }
        },
....
```


**Describe the results you received:**

```
"Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "[/dummy-entrypoint]"
            ],
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
"Entrypoint": [
                "/dummy-entrypoint"
            ],
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.9.2-2.fc30.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
 buildah version
Version:         1.9.2
Go Version:      go1.12.7
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

```
**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Fedora release 30 (Thirty)
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="30 (Workstation Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=30
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f30"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 30 (Workstation Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:30"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f30/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=30
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=30
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Workstation Edition"
VARIANT_ID=workstation
Fedora release 30 (Thirty)
Fedora release 30 (Thirty)

```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux jtudelag-t480s 5.1.17-300.fc30.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Jul 10 15:20:27 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

```

comments.body: I think the shell dropped the `"` from the entry point value before passing it to buildah, which then didn't recognize the value it was passed as an array.  Can you quote it using `'` (i.e., `'["/dummy-entrypoint]"]`) and see if that works better?

You are right @nalind , if I use simple quotes `'["/dummy-entrypoint"]'`, it works.

On the other hand, for Dockerfiles, no single quotes are needed...
The following Dockerfile sets the entrypoint correctly.

```
FROM scratch
ENTRYPOINT ["/hello"]
```

It is a bit confusing having different behaviours when using a Dockerfile or a Buildah native script.

Is this expected? Any chances of having a consistent behaviour between the two?

Thanks!

@jtudelag The issue is you are dealing with bash interpreting the command via shell expansion before it ever gets to the tool.  Bash modifies the Entrypoint option.

```
$ echo ["/dummy-entrypoint"]
[/dummy-entrypoint]
$ echo '["/dummy-entrypoint"]'
["/dummy-entrypoint"]
```

@rhatdan understood!!! Thanks for your answer.

Closing the issue then ;).
